sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, value DOUBLE, description TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value, description, created_at) VALUES (1, 2.5, 'test record 1', NOW()), (2, 5.0, 'test record 2', NOW()), (3, NULL, 'test record 3', NOW());
SELECT id, power(value, 3) AS power_value, description FROM test_data WHERE power(value, 2) < 20;
SELECT id, value FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL AND value < (SELECT power(2, 4) FROM test_data WHERE id = 1);
SELECT AVG(power(value, 2)) AS avg_power FROM test_data GROUP BY description HAVING AVG(power(value, 2)) < 50;
SELECT td1.id, td1.value FROM test_data td1 JOIN test_data td2 ON power(td1.value, 2) < td2.value WHERE td2.description IS NOT NULL;
SELECT description FROM test_data WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NULL) < (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days';
WITH power_calc AS (SELECT id, power(value, 1.5) AS calc_value FROM test_data) SELECT * FROM power_calc WHERE calc_value < (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_data) ORDER BY calc_value DESC;
SELECT id, power(COALESCE(value, 1), 2) AS adjusted_value FROM test_data WHERE adjusted_value < 15;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE additional_data (ref_id INT, score VARCHAR(10));
SELECT ad.ref_id, power(NULLIF(ad.score::DOUBLE PRECISION, NULL), 2) FROM additional_data ad JOIN test_data td ON ad.ref_id = td.id WHERE td.value < power(2, 3);
